mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals stronger household demand and near-term revenue momentum, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS=353866.0 as of one month before decision
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day return Rank IC above 0.03 in at least 60% of rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate above a restrictive threshold increases equity discount rates and tightens financing conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0% and its 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS=4.0 as of one month before decision
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with a falling unemployment rate identify broad labor-market strengthening that supports income growth and consumption, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE=5.0 as of one month before decision
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition fails to outperform the unconditional 60-day return mean by at least 50 bps after transaction-cost-free walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving real output and operating leverage for listed firms, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO=108.96 as of one month before decision
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO momentum has a nonpositive coefficient in walk-forward return regressions or if its 60-day hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices without a matching rise in the policy rate compresses real purchasing power and raises inflation-risk premia, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its 12-month growth while FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL=197.8 as of one month before decision
regime_hint: inflation scare with policy behind the curve
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-acceleration episodes have median 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than zero or if the interaction term is not negative in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening construction demand, household balance-sheet stress, and slower credit creation, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average after falling more than 10% over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST=2014.0 as of two months before decision
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or show no negative average excess return.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarterly declines in real GDP growth signal broad demand deceleration and earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth crosses below its prior-quarter growth for two consecutive releases
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1=11206.1 as of five months before decision
failure_condition: Reject if two-quarter GDPC1 deceleration episodes do not have lower 60-day forward returns than non-trigger periods with p-value <= 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals rising recession risk and weaker household income, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as risk premia adjust quickly.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses upward by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE=5.0 as of one month before decision
failure_condition: Reject if upward UNRATE shock triggers have average 5-day S&P 500 returns greater than zero or if downside hit rate is below 55% in out-of-sample testing.
